content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 25, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on June 25 focused on LifeOps optimization, cross-platform stability for Windows, and rigorous testing of cloud-billing and benchmark systems. Key achievements include live GEPA optimization for LifeOps actions, critical bug fixes for Windows-based shell and path handling, and a comprehensive audit of the benchmark suite to ensure high-fidelity performance evaluation.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

### LifeOps Optimization & Audit
*   Implemented live GEPA optimization for LifeOps actions on `gpt-oss-120b` and established a scheduling-negotiation planner seed ([#9543](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9543), [#9531](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9531)).
*   Conducted a 10-subsystem audit of LifeOps, resulting in 53 verified findings and improved scoring coverage for GEPA scorers ([#9545](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9545), [#9547](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9547)).
*   Fixed hallucinated Gmail subactions and resolved relative date parsing issues in the calendar handler ([#9548](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9548), [#9546](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9546)).

### Cross-Platform & Infrastructure Stability
*   Resolved multiple Windows-specific issues, including shell-based spawning for npm/Bitwarden CLI, renderer URL fallbacks, and cross-platform source searching ([#9542](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9542), [#9541](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9541), [#9537](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9537), [#9534](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9534), [#9532](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9532), [#9530](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9530), [#9527](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9527)).
*   Addressed a live production outage by preventing pricing-catalog fetch failures from triggering 500 errors ([#9539](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9539)).
*   Updated Android fused-lib build pins and implemented a fail-closed gate for Mali GPU flash-attention mitigations ([#9533](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9533), [#9528](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9528)).

### Testing & Benchmark Refinement
*   Performed a major "de-larp" of the benchmark suite, removing unwired benchmarks and implementing CI-coverage gates ([#9549](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9549)).
*   Expanded test coverage for cloud-shared services, specifically targeting app-credits billing, money-safety branches, and PGlite reconciliation ([#9553](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9553), [#9551](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9551), [#9535](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9535)).
*   Refactored UI components to use canonical activity serialization ([#9557](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9557)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#9556](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9556): Raise credit-markup ceiling.
*   [#9555](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9555): Cover web workspace command flow.
*   [#9554](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9554): Cover notification category filter.
*   [#9552](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9552): Build model plugins before example typecheck.
*   [#9550](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9550): Filter notifications by category.
*   [#9544](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9544): Type mock llm body reader.
*   [#9538](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9538): Wire workspace error codes.
*   [#9526](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/9526): Sample-level AEC for live barge-in.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   *No issues were explicitly marked as closed in the provided activity data.*

## NEW ISSUES
